stories.headline: Waiting for Hassan: Another Gaza doctor held by Israel without charge
stories.summary: Hassan Khalil Almukayed is among at least 15 Palestinian doctors from Gaza currently in Israeli detention.

stories.headline: U.S. War Against Iran Enters a New Phase
stories.summary: As President Trump resumes his war, the focus is now on the Strait of Hormuz. But it remains unclear how far the U.S. military will go to exert control.

stories.headline: Von der Leyen arrives in Kyiv to discuss drones and EU accession with Zelenskyy
stories.summary: The visit of the president of the European Commission to Kyiv will have a heavy focus on drone production and EU accession.

stories.headline: Newsletter: ‘Tide turning’ for Ukraine, von der Leyen says in Kyiv
stories.summary: Hello, it’s Wednesday, and I’m Mared Gwyn. In today’s newsletter: the view on the ground in Ukraine as European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen visits, and a new EU-UK pact marks a “new era” for Gibraltar.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Ukraine at a turning point as Europe steps up support
stories.summary: Ukraine is facing a political reset as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy reshuffles his government amid intensified Russian attacks. The EU prepares fresh support for Kyiv as Ursula von der Leyen visits, while food security, Gibraltar’s post-Brexit future and Cyprus tensions remain in focus.

stories.headline: Lithuania warns Russia could be planning ‘targeted’ attacks on critical infrastructure – Europe live
stories.summary: Lithuania’s president says Russia could be looking to escalate against one of the Baltic countries or PolandBrussels correspondentA decision on the EU’s next round of sanctions against Russia is going to the wire. Again.“Our aim is to have an agreement, If we don’t have an agreement we start to work on plan B.” Continue reading...

stories.headline: How the failed 2016 coup reshaped Turkiye’s civil-military relations

stories.headline: China's economic growth hits slowest pace in more than three years
stories.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% year on year in the second quarter, its weakest pace since late 2022, as weak domestic demand and a prolonged property downturn outweighed strong exports, increasing pressure on Beijing to provide more economic support.

Europe's biggest company ASML lifts forecast, fuelling fresh hopes for the AI boom
stories.summary: Europe's biggest company by market capitalisation, ASML, continues to benefit from the AI boom after raising its full-year net sales forecast to between €43bn and €45bn. Investors welcomed the upgrade, sending the shares up more than 5%.

stories.headline: Can batteries save the EU's wasted renewable energy?
stories.summary: The EU's first tripartite agreement on energy storage, signed in June, puts batteries at the heart of plans to capture more solar and wind power. The technology is ready and getting cheaper, but market rules, grid delays, industrial policy and raw-material politics still hold it back.

stories.headline: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
stories.summary: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

stories.headline: At War With Iran Again, Trump Finds an Opponent He Cannot Easily Dominate
stories.summary: The president is accustomed to forcing other countries to bend to his will. But he is struggling to come up with a strategy to extract Iranian concessions as the cease-fire he brokered collapses.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Sister, Darline Graham, Is Sworn In to Finish His Senate Term

stories.headline: Trump pays writer E Jean Carroll $5m in damages over sexual abuse and defamation

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to serve the rest of his Senate term

stories.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
